items.base_claim: During learning-rate warmup, replace the linear ramp with a power-law ramp in which the learning rate rises as (t/T_w)^p over a window of length T_w, treating the exponent p together with the peak learning rate and the warmup length as hyperparameters selected jointly by grid search at each model scale, leaving the post-warmup decay phase unchanged. Evaluate by comparing final validation perplexity and training stability against the linear-warmup baseline across several model sizes.
items.sig_claim: During learning-rate warmup, replace the linear ramp with a power-law ramp in which the learning rate rises as (t/T_w)^p over a window of length T_w, treating the exponent p together with the peak learning rate and the warmup length as hyperparameters selected jointly by grid search at each model scale, leaving the post-warmup decay phase unchanged. Evaluate by comparing final validation perplexity and training stability against the linear-warmup baseline across several model sizes.
items.pad_claim: During learning-rate warmup, replace the linear ramp with a power-law ramp in which the learning rate rises as (t/T_w)^p over a window of length T_w, treating the exponent p together with the peak learning rate and the warmup length as hyperparameters selected jointly by grid search at each model scale, leaving the post-warmup decay phase unchanged. Evaluate by comparing final validation perplexity and training stability against the linear-warmup baseline across several model sizes.

items.base_claim: In an AdamW-style decoupled weight-decay optimizer, divide the weight-decay term by the current learning rate normalized against a reference value so the learning-rate schedule no longer scales the realized per-step regularization, and give the weight-decay coefficient its own separately tuned schedule; the moment estimates, adaptive step, and optimizer state are left unchanged, so the learning-rate schedule and weight-decay schedule are tuned as two independent curves.
items.sig_claim: In an AdamW-style decoupled weight-decay optimizer, divide the weight-decay term by the current learning rate normalized against a reference value so the learning-rate schedule no longer scales the realized per-step regularization, and give the weight-decay coefficient its own separately tuned schedule; the moment estimates, adaptive step, and optimizer state are left unchanged, so the learning-rate schedule and weight-decay schedule are tuned as two independent curves.
items.pad_claim: In an AdamW-style decoupled weight-decay optimizer, divide the weight-decay term by the current learning rate normalized against a reference value so the learning-rate schedule no longer scales the realized per-step regularization, and give the weight-decay coefficient its own separately tuned schedule; the moment estimates, adaptive step, and optimizer state are left unchanged, so the learning-rate schedule and weight-decay schedule are tuned as two independent curves.

items.base_claim: Store the Lion optimizer's single momentum buffer in a low-precision block-wise quantized format (e.g. 8-bit per element with per-block scaling factors) instead of 16/32-bit floating point, dequantizing the buffer to compute the sign-momentum update and requantizing after each step, leaving the update rule unchanged and altering only the storage representation. Evaluate by comparing training curves, final accuracy, and memory footprint against full-precision Lion and 8-bit Adam.
items.sig_claim: Store the Lion optimizer's single momentum buffer in a low-precision block-wise quantized format (e.g. 8-bit per element with per-block scaling factors) instead of 16/32-bit floating point, dequantizing the buffer to compute the sign-momentum update and requantizing after each step, leaving the update rule unchanged and altering only the storage representation. Evaluate by comparing training curves, final accuracy, and memory footprint against full-precision Lion and 8-bit Adam.
items.pad_claim: Store the Lion optimizer's single momentum buffer in a low-precision block-wise quantized format (e.g. 8-bit per element with per-block scaling factors) instead of 16/32-bit floating point, dequantizing the buffer to compute the sign-momentum update and requantizing after each step, leaving the update rule unchanged and altering only the storage representation. Evaluate by comparing training curves, final accuracy, and memory footprint against full-precision Lion and 8-bit Adam.

items.base_claim: Apply a whitening transform (subtract a corpus mean, then apply a covariance-based linear map, optionally reducing dimension) to sentence embeddings from a contrastively trained encoder rather than a masked-language-model encoder, replacing the empirical covariance with a shrinkage estimate that interpolates between the sample covariance and a scaled identity, with the shrinkage coefficient chosen on held-out data. Estimate the centering vector and whitening matrix once over the target corpus, apply them as a fixed linear post-processing step, and measure performance on standard semantic-similarity benchmarks against whitening that uses the unregularized covariance.
items.sig_claim: Apply a whitening transform (subtract a corpus mean, then apply a covariance-based linear map, optionally reducing dimension) to sentence embeddings from a contrastively trained encoder rather than a masked-language-model encoder, replacing the empirical covariance with a shrinkage estimate that interpolates between the sample covariance and a scaled identity, with the shrinkage coefficient chosen on held-out data. Estimate the centering vector and whitening matrix once over the target corpus, apply them as a fixed linear post-processing step, and measure performance on standard semantic-similarity benchmarks against whitening that uses the unregularized covariance.
items.pad_claim: Apply a whitening transform (subtract a corpus mean, then apply a covariance-based linear map, optionally reducing dimension) to sentence embeddings from a contrastively trained encoder rather than a masked-language-model encoder, replacing the empirical covariance with a shrinkage estimate that interpolates between the sample covariance and a scaled identity, with the shrinkage coefficient chosen on held-out data. Estimate the centering vector and whitening matrix once over the target corpus, apply them as a fixed linear post-processing step, and measure performance on standard semantic-similarity benchmarks against whitening that uses the unregularized covariance.

items.base_claim: Apply VICReg's variance regularization (a per-dimension hinge keeping each coordinate's standard deviation above a floor) and covariance regularization (an off-diagonal penalty decorrelating the dimensions) at several intermediate backbone layers in addition to the projector output, using depth-specific weights tuned on a validation split and leaving the invariance term unchanged. Evaluate by per-layer effective rank and downstream linear-probe accuracy.
items.sig_claim: Apply VICReg's variance regularization (a per-dimension hinge keeping each coordinate's standard deviation above a floor) and covariance regularization (an off-diagonal penalty decorrelating the dimensions) at several intermediate backbone layers in addition to the projector output, using depth-specific weights tuned on a validation split and leaving the invariance term unchanged. Evaluate by per-layer effective rank and downstream linear-probe accuracy.
items.pad_claim: Apply VICReg's variance regularization (a per-dimension hinge keeping each coordinate's standard deviation above a floor) and covariance regularization (an off-diagonal penalty decorrelating the dimensions) at several intermediate backbone layers in addition to the projector output, using depth-specific weights tuned on a validation split and leaving the invariance term unchanged. Evaluate by per-layer effective rank and downstream linear-probe accuracy.

items.base_claim: During fine-tuning of a pretrained model, apply layer-wise learning-rate decay that gives the feature extractor smaller learning rates and the classification head larger ones, bounding how far pretrained features move. Sweep the feature-extractor learning rate and measure in-distribution and out-of-distribution accuracy across several shift magnitudes and datasets, tracing the ID-OOD accuracy frontier and comparing it against standard fine-tuning and linear-probe-then-fine-tune.
items.sig_claim: During fine-tuning of a pretrained model, apply layer-wise learning-rate decay that gives the feature extractor smaller learning rates and the classification head larger ones, bounding how far pretrained features move. Sweep the feature-extractor learning rate and measure in-distribution and out-of-distribution accuracy across several shift magnitudes and datasets, tracing the ID-OOD accuracy frontier and comparing it against standard fine-tuning and linear-probe-then-fine-tune.
items.pad_claim: During fine-tuning of a pretrained model, apply layer-wise learning-rate decay that gives the feature extractor smaller learning rates and the classification head larger ones, bounding how far pretrained features move. Sweep the feature-extractor learning rate and measure in-distribution and out-of-distribution accuracy across several shift magnitudes and datasets, tracing the ID-OOD accuracy frontier and comparing it against standard fine-tuning and linear-probe-then-fine-tune.

items.base_claim: Train a SimSiam-style two-view architecture with gradients flowing symmetrically through both branches (no stop-gradient, no momentum encoder), and prevent collapse by adding a regularizer that drives the batch-wise feature covariance toward the identity: a hinge penalty keeping each dimension's variance above a floor plus an off-diagonal covariance penalty, applied identically to both views. The regularizer weight is a tunable hyperparameter and the predictor head is optional.
items.sig_claim: Train a SimSiam-style two-view architecture with gradients flowing symmetrically through both branches (no stop-gradient, no momentum encoder), and prevent collapse by adding a regularizer that drives the batch-wise feature covariance toward the identity: a hinge penalty keeping each dimension's variance above a floor plus an off-diagonal covariance penalty, applied identically to both views. The regularizer weight is a tunable hyperparameter and the predictor head is optional.
items.pad_claim: Train a SimSiam-style two-view architecture with gradients flowing symmetrically through both branches (no stop-gradient, no momentum encoder), and prevent collapse by adding a regularizer that drives the batch-wise feature covariance toward the identity: a hinge penalty keeping each dimension's variance above a floor plus an off-diagonal covariance penalty, applied identically to both views. The regularizer weight is a tunable hyperparameter and the predictor head is optional.

items.base_claim: In Soft Actor-Critic, replace the single scalar entropy temperature with a vector of temperature coefficients, one per action dimension, each adjusted independently by dual gradient descent to meet its own per-dimension target entropy (e.g. -1 per dimension, which sums to the standard aggregate target). The policy update weights each entropy term by its per-dimension coefficient; the maximum-entropy objective, critic updates, and dual-descent procedure are otherwise unchanged.
items.sig_claim: In Soft Actor-Critic, replace the single scalar entropy temperature with a vector of temperature coefficients, one per action dimension, each adjusted independently by dual gradient descent to meet its own per-dimension target entropy (e.g. -1 per dimension, which sums to the standard aggregate target). The policy update weights each entropy term by its per-dimension coefficient; the maximum-entropy objective, critic updates, and dual-descent procedure are otherwise unchanged.
items.pad_claim: In Soft Actor-Critic, replace the single scalar entropy temperature with a vector of temperature coefficients, one per action dimension, each adjusted independently by dual gradient descent to meet its own per-dimension target entropy (e.g. -1 per dimension, which sums to the standard aggregate target). The policy update weights each entropy term by its per-dimension coefficient; the maximum-entropy objective, critic updates, and dual-descent procedure are otherwise unchanged.

items.base_claim: In TD3's clipped double-Q learning, replace the minimum of the two critic targets with a convex combination of their minimum and their mean, governed by a single scalar coefficient that interpolates between the hard minimum and the mean and is selected per environment by validation return. All other TD3 components (twin critics, target policy smoothing, delayed policy updates, and selection/evaluation decoupling) are retained unchanged.
items.sig_claim: In TD3's clipped double-Q learning, replace the minimum of the two critic targets with a convex combination of their minimum and their mean, governed by a single scalar coefficient that interpolates between the hard minimum and the mean and is selected per environment by validation return. All other TD3 components (twin critics, target policy smoothing, delayed policy updates, and selection/evaluation decoupling) are retained unchanged.
items.pad_claim: In TD3's clipped double-Q learning, replace the minimum of the two critic targets with a convex combination of their minimum and their mean, governed by a single scalar coefficient that interpolates between the hard minimum and the mean and is selected per environment by validation return. All other TD3 components (twin critics, target policy smoothing, delayed policy updates, and selection/evaluation decoupling) are retained unchanged.

items.base_claim: In PPO, replace the fixed clip parameter of the surrogate objective with a scalar adjusted online by a feedback controller: each iteration measure the empirical KL divergence between the previous and updated policy against a target, enlarging the clip parameter (trust-region size) when KL falls below target and reducing it when KL exceeds target. Target KL and controller gain are hyperparameters; advantage estimation and the surrogate form are unchanged.
items.sig_claim: In PPO, replace the fixed clip parameter of the surrogate objective with a scalar adjusted online by a feedback controller: each iteration measure the empirical KL divergence between the previous and updated policy against a target, enlarging the clip parameter (trust-region size) when KL falls below target and reducing it when KL exceeds target. Target KL and controller gain are hyperparameters; advantage estimation and the surrogate form are unchanged.
items.pad_claim: In PPO, replace the fixed clip parameter of the surrogate objective with a scalar adjusted online by a feedback controller: each iteration measure the empirical KL divergence between the previous and updated policy against a target, enlarging the clip parameter (trust-region size) when KL falls below target and reducing it when KL exceeds target. Target KL and controller gain are hyperparameters; advantage estimation and the surrogate form are unchanged.

items.base_claim: Make the Conservative Q-Learning penalty coefficient state-dependent: at each state estimate local data density or epistemic uncertainty (via ensemble disagreement or a kernel-density model over the offline dataset) and scale the penalty that lowers out-of-distribution action-values in proportion to that uncertainty, so sparsely-covered states get a stronger penalty and well-covered states a weaker one. The CQL value lower-bound and overall training objective are unchanged; only the fixed conservatism weight becomes a per-state coverage-tied schedule.
items.sig_claim: Make the Conservative Q-Learning penalty coefficient state-dependent: at each state estimate local data density or epistemic uncertainty (via ensemble disagreement or a kernel-density model over the offline dataset) and scale the penalty on out-of-distribution action-values in proportion to that uncertainty, so sparsely-covered states get a stronger penalty and well-covered states a weaker one. The CQL value lower-bound and overall training objective are unchanged; only the fixed conservatism weight becomes a per-state coverage-tied schedule.
items.pad_claim: Make the Conservative Q-Learning penalty coefficient state-dependent: at each state estimate local data density or epistemic uncertainty (via ensemble disagreement or a kernel-density estimate over the offline dataset) and scale the penalty on out-of-distribution action-values in proportion to that uncertainty, so sparsely-covered states get a stronger penalty and well-covered states a weaker one. The CQL value lower-bound and overall training objective are unchanged; only the fixed conservatism weight becomes a per-state coverage-tied schedule.

items.base_claim: During RLHF PPO training, fit Gao et al.'s reward-overoptimization curve online from a small held-out set of gold-labeled prompts to estimate the KL distance at which projected gold reward peaks, and use a feedback controller to set and adjust the KL penalty coefficient toward that estimated turnover point (with larger KL allowance for prompt clusters whose fitted turnover occurs later). Evaluate against fixed-coefficient PPO on gold reward at matched compute.
items.sig_claim: During RLHF PPO training, fit Gao et al.'s reward-overoptimization scaling-law form online from a small held-out set of gold-labeled prompts to estimate the KL distance at which projected gold reward peaks, and use a feedback controller to set and adjust the KL penalty coefficient toward that estimated turnover point (with larger KL allowance for prompt clusters whose fitted turnover occurs later). Evaluate against fixed-coefficient PPO on gold reward at matched compute.
items.pad_claim: During RLHF PPO training, fit Gao et al.'s reward-overoptimization curve online from a small held-out panel of gold-labeled prompts to estimate the KL distance at which projected gold reward is maximized, and use a feedback controller to set and adjust the KL penalty coefficient toward that estimated turnover point (with larger KL allowance for prompt clusters whose fitted turnover occurs later). Evaluate against fixed-coefficient PPO on gold reward at matched compute.

items.base_claim: Replace Random Network Distillation's single predictor-target pair with an ensemble of K independently initialized predictor-target pairs and define the intrinsic novelty bonus as the mean prediction error across the ensemble, normalized by a running estimate of its standard deviation, while linearly annealing the intrinsic-reward coefficient toward zero over training. Otherwise identical to standard RND, adding two hyperparameters: ensemble size and anneal schedule.
items.sig_claim: Replace Random Network Distillation's single predictor-target pair with an ensemble of K independently initialized predictor-target pairs and define the intrinsic novelty bonus as the mean prediction error across the ensemble, normalized by a running estimate of its standard deviation, while linearly annealing the intrinsic-reward coefficient toward zero over training. Otherwise identical to standard RND, adding two hyperparameters: ensemble size and anneal schedule.
items.pad_claim: Replace Random Network Distillation's single predictor-target pair with an ensemble of K independently initialized predictor-target pairs and define the intrinsic novelty bonus as the mean prediction error across the ensemble, normalized by a running estimate of its standard deviation, while linearly annealing the intrinsic-reward coefficient toward zero over training. Otherwise identical to standard RND, adding two hyperparameters: ensemble size and anneal schedule.

items.base_claim: During conditional flow-matching training, weight each velocity-field regression target by an estimate of the inverse local data density at its data endpoint (a k-nearest-neighbor or kernel density estimate on a held-out data subset, with a tunable weighting exponent), so samples in low-density/under-represented modes contribute proportionally larger gradients. Applied on top of standard flow matching and rectified flow with the ODE sampler unchanged; evaluated by recall and captured-mode counts.
items.sig_claim: During conditional flow-matching training, weight each velocity-field regression target by an estimate of the inverse local data density at its data endpoint (a k-nearest-neighbor or kernel density estimate on a held-out data subset, with a single tunable weighting exponent), so samples in low-density/under-represented modes contribute proportionally larger gradients. Applied on top of standard flow matching and rectified flow with the ODE sampler unchanged; evaluated by recall and captured-mode counts.
items.pad_claim: During conditional flow-matching training, reweight each velocity-field regression target by an estimate of the inverse local data density at its data endpoint (a k-nearest-neighbor or kernel density estimate over a held-out data subset, with a single tunable weighting exponent), so samples from sparse/under-represented modes exert proportionally larger gradients. Applied on top of standard flow matching and rectified flow with the deterministic ODE integrator unchanged; evaluated by recall and captured-mode counts.

items.base_claim: Replace the uniform L1 sparsity penalty of a sparse autoencoder with a per-atom penalty weighted by an estimate of each feature's importance (the variance an atom explains in downstream activations or its measured effect on the loss), so more important atoms receive a smaller penalty and are less likely to be suppressed. Train on residual-stream activations of a small transformer; evaluate against known ground-truth features in the toy setting and by reconstruction and interpretability metrics.
items.sig_claim: Replace the uniform L1 sparsity penalty of a sparse autoencoder with a per-atom penalty weighted by an estimate of each feature's importance (the variance an atom explains in downstream activations or its measured effect on the loss), so more important atoms receive a smaller penalty and are less likely to be suppressed. Train on residual-stream activations of a small transformer; evaluate against known ground-truth features in the toy setting and by reconstruction and interpretability metrics.
items.pad_claim: Replace the uniform L1 sparsity penalty of a sparse autoencoder (encoder-decoder) with a per-atom penalty discounted in proportion to an estimate of each atom's importance (the variance an atom accounts for in downstream activations or its measured contribution to the loss), so more important atoms receive a smaller penalty and are less likely to be dropped. Fit to residual-stream activations of a small transformer; evaluate against known ground-truth generative factors in the toy setting and by reconstruction and interpretability metrics.

items.base_claim: Define a generalized prefix-matching score for induction heads that credits soft matches based on token-embedding similarity and short n-gram context instead of exact single-token identity, and label heads scoring high as approximate-copying heads. Measure their causal contribution to few-shot in-context accuracy by ablating them on inputs with paraphrased or noisy repetitions, and track when they emerge relative to the in-context-learning phase transition during training.
items.sig_claim: Define a generalized prefix-matching score for induction heads that credits soft matches based on token-embedding similarity and short n-gram context instead of exact single-token identity, and label heads scoring high as approximate-copying heads. Measure their causal contribution to few-shot in-context accuracy by ablating them on inputs with paraphrased or noisy repetitions, and track when they emerge relative to the in-context-learning phase transition during training.
items.pad_claim: Define a generalized prefix-matching score for induction heads that credits soft matches based on token-embedding similarity and short n-gram context instead of exact single-token identity, and label heads scoring high as approximate-copying heads. Measure their causal contribution to few-shot in-context accuracy by ablating them on inputs with paraphrased or noisy repetitions, and track when they emerge relative to the in-context-learning phase transition during training.

items.base_claim: Run the corrupt-and-restore causal tracing protocol (corrupt subject tokens with Gaussian noise, then restore clean activations at each layer and token position and measure the indirect effect on the correct object's probability) over a larger set of factual relations and across several model sizes. Map how the peak mediating layer and token position vary with relation type, relation frequency, and model scale, and test whether GPT-2's mid-layer MLP localization still holds.
items.sig_claim: Run the corrupt-and-restore causal tracing protocol (corrupt subject tokens with Gaussian noise, then restore clean activations at each layer and token position and measure the indirect effect on the correct object's probability) over a larger set of factual relations and across several model sizes. Map how the peak mediating layer and token position vary with relation type, relation frequency, and model scale, and test whether GPT-2's mid-layer MLP localization still holds.
items.pad_claim: Run the corrupt-and-restore causal tracing protocol (corrupt subject tokens with Gaussian noise, then restore clean activations at each layer and token position and measure the indirect effect on the correct object's probability) over a larger set of factual relations and across several model sizes. Map how the peak mediating layer and token position vary with relation type, relation frequency, and model scale, and test whether GPT-2's mid-layer MLP localization still holds.

items.base_claim: In a sparse autoencoder on transformer residual-stream activations, replace the single global L1 sparsity coefficient with a per-feature coefficient adapted online to equalize activation frequency across features, and add a decorrelation penalty between decoder columns, keeping the standard reconstruction-plus-sparsity objective and dead-feature resampling. Sweep the sparsity target across residual-stream layers and compare reconstruction fidelity and automated interpretability at matched L0 against the fixed-coefficient baseline.
items.sig_claim: In a sparse autoencoder on transformer residual-stream activations, replace the single global L1 sparsity coefficient with a per-feature coefficient adapted online to equalize activation frequency across features, and add a decorrelation penalty between decoder columns, keeping the standard reconstruction-plus-sparsity objective and dead-feature resampling. Sweep the sparsity target across residual-stream layers and compare reconstruction fidelity and automated interpretability at matched L0 against the fixed-coefficient baseline.
items.pad_claim: In a sparse autoencoder on transformer residual-stream activations, replace the single global L1 sparsity coefficient with a per-feature coefficient adapted online to equalize activation frequency across features, and add a decorrelation penalty between decoder columns, keeping the standard reconstruction-plus-sparsity objective and dead-feature resampling. Sweep the sparsity target across residual-stream layers and compare reconstruction fidelity and automated interpretability at matched L0 against the fixed-coefficient baseline.

items.base_claim: Modify attention rollout by scaling each layer's attention matrix by the L2 norm of that layer's transformed value vectors before the multiplicative cross-layer aggregation. Evaluate the resulting token attributions with deletion and insertion perturbation curves on faithfulness benchmarks and against gradient-times-input across several pretrained transformer classifiers.
items.sig_claim: Modify attention rollout by scaling each layer's attention matrix by the L2 norm of that layer's transformed value vectors before the multiplicative cross-layer aggregation. Evaluate the resulting token attributions with deletion and insertion perturbation curves on faithfulness benchmarks and against gradient-times-input across several pretrained transformer classifiers.
items.pad_claim: Modify attention rollout by scaling each layer's attention matrix by the L2 norm of that layer's transformed value vectors before the multiplicative cross-layer aggregation. Evaluate the resulting token attributions with deletion and insertion perturbation curves on faithfulness benchmarks and against gradient-times-input across several pretrained transformer classifiers.

items.base_claim: Distribute a fixed total KV-cache budget across attention heads and layers adaptively, using an online estimate of each head's attention entropy so that heads with concentrated attention get smaller budgets and heads with dispersed attention get larger budgets while the total is held constant. Within each head, still select which key/value entries to keep by the standard eviction rule (accumulated attention score plus a recency window); only the per-head budget split is made adaptive.
items.sig_claim: Distribute a fixed total KV-cache budget across attention heads and layers adaptively, using an online estimate of each head's attention entropy so that heads with concentrated attention get smaller budgets and heads with dispersed attention get larger budgets while the total is held constant. Within each head, still select which key/value entries to keep by the standard eviction rule (accumulated attention score plus a recency window); only the per-head budget split is made adaptive.
items.pad_claim: Distribute a fixed total KV-cache budget across attention heads and layers adaptively, using an online estimate of each head's attention entropy so that heads with concentrated attention get smaller budgets and heads with dispersed attention get larger budgets while the total is held constant. Within each head, still select which key/value entries to keep by the standard eviction rule (accumulated attention score plus a recency window); only the per-head budget split is made adaptive.

items.base_claim: In speculative decoding, adaptively set the number of tokens drafted before each verification step using an online controller that tracks an exponential moving average of the recent per-step accepted-token count, drafting more when recent acceptance is high and fewer when low, so as to minimize expected wasted computation. The draft and target models, the rejection-sampling acceptance test, and the exact output distribution are unchanged; only the per-step speculation length becomes adaptive rather than fixed.
items.sig_claim: In speculative decoding, adaptively set the number of tokens drafted before each verification step using an online controller that tracks an exponential moving average of the recent per-step accepted-token count, drafting more when recent acceptance is high and fewer when low, so as to minimize expected wasted computation. The draft and target models, the rejection-sampling acceptance test, and the exact output distribution are unchanged; only the per-step speculation length becomes adaptive rather than fixed.
items.pad_claim: In speculative decoding, adaptively set the number of tokens drafted before each verification step using an online controller that tracks an exponential moving average of the recent per-step accepted-token count, drafting more when recent acceptance is high and fewer when low, so as to minimize expected wasted computation. The draft and target models, the rejection-sampling acceptance test, and the exact output distribution are unchanged; only the per-step speculation length becomes adaptive rather than fixed.

items.base_claim: Before invoking an existing variable-length FlashAttention kernel, sort each batch's sequences into length buckets and reorder the packed buffer accordingly (storing an inverse permutation to restore the original order after the kernel returns), so that concurrently launched query-blocks carry more uniform work and load imbalance across GPU streaming multiprocessors is reduced. The attention kernel, its tiling, and its online-softmax computation are unchanged; only host-side packing order and an index remapping are added, and outputs remain exact.
items.sig_claim: Before invoking an existing variable-length FlashAttention kernel, sort each batch's sequences into length buckets and reorder the packed buffer accordingly (storing an inverse permutation to restore the original order after the kernel returns), so that concurrently launched query-blocks carry more uniform work and load imbalance across GPU streaming multiprocessors is reduced. The attention kernel, its tiling, and its online-softmax computation are unchanged; only host-side packing order and an index remapping are added, and outputs remain exact.
items.pad_claim: Before invoking an existing variable-length FlashAttention kernel, sort each batch's sequences into length buckets and reorder the packed buffer accordingly (storing an inverse permutation to restore the original order after the kernel returns), so that concurrently launched query-blocks carry more uniform work and load imbalance across GPU streaming multiprocessors is reduced. The attention kernel, its tiling, and its online-softmax computation are unchanged; only host-side packing order and an index remapping are added, and outputs remain exact.

items.base_claim: Define a drift statistic equal to the relative Frobenius-norm change of the empirical neural tangent kernel between initialization and convergence; measure it across a range of network widths and standard architectures, fit its dependence on width, and test whether it decreases at approximately an inverse-width rate. Then test whether larger drift accompanies a larger gap between the test error predicted by kernel regression using the initial NTK and the actual test error of the trained finite-width network.
items.sig_claim: Define a drift statistic equal to the relative Frobenius-norm change of the empirical neural tangent kernel between initialization and convergence; measure it across a range of network widths and standard architectures, fit its dependence on width, and test whether it decreases at approximately an inverse-width rate. Then test whether larger drift accompanies a larger gap between the test error predicted by kernel regression using the initial NTK and the actual test error of the trained finite-width network.
items.pad_claim: Define a drift statistic equal to the relative Frobenius-norm change of the empirical neural tangent kernel between initialization and convergence; measure it across a range of network widths and standard architectures, fit its dependence on width, and test whether it decreases at approximately an inverse-width rate. Then test whether larger drift accompanies a larger gap between the test error predicted by kernel regression using the initial NTK and the actual test error of the trained finite-width network.

items.base_claim: Prove that gradient descent with constant heavy-ball momentum on linearly separable data under exponentially-tailed losses (e.g. logistic loss) has its normalized iterate converge in direction to the L2 max-margin (hard-margin SVM) solution, with the momentum coefficient entering only the rate constants and lower-order terms while leaving the limiting direction and the O(1/log t) convergence order unchanged, and give conditions on the step size and momentum coefficient under which the loss tends to zero and the direction converges.
items.sig_claim: Prove that gradient descent with constant heavy-ball momentum on linearly separable data under exponentially-tailed losses (e.g. logistic loss) has its normalized iterate converge in direction to the L2 max-margin (hard-margin SVM) solution, with the momentum coefficient entering only the rate constants and lower-order terms while leaving the limiting direction and the O(1/log t) convergence order unchanged, and give conditions on the step size and momentum coefficient under which the loss tends to zero and the direction converges.
items.pad_claim: Prove that gradient descent with constant heavy-ball momentum on linearly separable data under exponentially-tailed losses (e.g. logistic loss) has its normalized iterate converge in direction to the L2 max-margin (hard-margin SVM) solution, with the momentum coefficient entering only the rate constants and lower-order terms while leaving the limiting direction and the O(1/log t) convergence order unchanged, and give conditions on the step size and momentum coefficient under which the loss tends to zero and the direction converges.

items.base_claim: Reformulate the Chinchilla compute-optimal allocation under a total-lifetime budget C_total = C_train + C_inf that adds expected inference cost (which grows with model size and with the number of tokens served) to training cost, then minimize the fitted loss L(N,D)=E+A/N^alpha+B/D^beta subject to this budget for a given expected query volume, and report how the compute-optimal token-per-parameter ratio shifts toward smaller models trained on more tokens as expected inference/deployment volume increases.
items.sig_claim: Reformulate the Chinchilla compute-optimal allocation under a total-lifetime budget C_total = C_train + C_inf that adds expected inference cost (which grows with model size and with the number of tokens served) to training cost, then minimize the fitted loss L(N,D)=E+A/N^alpha+B/D^beta subject to this budget for a given expected query volume, and report how the compute-optimal token-per-parameter ratio shifts toward smaller models trained on more tokens as expected inference/deployment volume increases.
items.pad_claim: Reformulate the Chinchilla compute-optimal allocation under a total-lifetime budget C_total = C_train + C_inf that adds expected inference cost (which grows with model size and with the number of tokens served) to training cost, then minimize the fitted loss L(N,D)=E+A/N^alpha+B/D^beta subject to this budget for a given expected query volume, and report how the compute-optimal token-per-parameter ratio shifts toward smaller models trained on more tokens as expected inference/deployment volume increases.

items.base_claim: Apply a fixed battery of input perturbations to language models producing chain-of-thought — reordering answer options, inserting a suggested answer, and rephrasing the question — and measure unfaithfulness as the rate at which these cues flip the final answer while never appearing in the stated reasoning. Report how this rate varies with model scale and task category across several model families.
items.sig_claim: Apply a fixed battery of input perturbations to language models producing chain-of-thought — reordering answer options, inserting a suggested answer, and rephrasing the question — and measure unfaithfulness as the rate at which these cues flip the final answer while never appearing in the stated reasoning. Report how this rate varies with model scale and task category across several model families.
items.pad_claim: Apply a fixed battery of input perturbations to language models producing chain-of-thought — reordering answer options, inserting a suggested answer, and rephrasing the question — and measure unfaithfulness as the rate at which these cues flip the final answer while never appearing in the stated reasoning. Report how this rate varies with model scale and task category across several model families.

items.base_claim: At a fixed training budget, embed and cluster instruction-tuning examples in a sentence-embedding space, then select a subset that maximizes the number of distinct clusters represented rather than sampling proportionally to cluster frequency. Compare this selection against uniform random sampling and against increasing the total example count at matched compute, measuring zero-shot generalization to held-out task families.
items.sig_claim: At a fixed training budget, embed and cluster instruction-tuning examples in a sentence-embedding space, then select a subset that maximizes the number of distinct clusters represented rather than sampling proportionally to cluster frequency. Compare this selection against uniform random sampling and against increasing the total example count at matched compute, measuring zero-shot generalization to held-out task families.
items.pad_claim: At a fixed training budget, embed and cluster instruction-tuning examples in a sentence-embedding space, then select a subset that maximizes the number of distinct clusters represented rather than sampling proportionally to cluster frequency. Compare this selection against uniform random sampling and against increasing the total example count at matched compute, measuring zero-shot generalization to held-out task families.

items.base_claim: Within the Bayesian-inference model of in-context learning, derive the rate at which the implicit posterior concentrates on the shared latent concept as a function of the divergence between concept-conditioned distributions in the pretraining mixture, predicting faster accuracy gains (fewer examples) when concepts are well separated. Add a hierarchical prior over concept families to model transfer, and evaluate the predictions on GINC-style synthetic data with controlled concept separation.
items.sig_claim: Within the Bayesian-inference model of in-context learning, derive the rate at which the implicit posterior concentrates on the shared latent concept as a function of the divergence between concept-conditioned distributions in the pretraining mixture, predicting faster accuracy gains (fewer examples) when concepts are well separated. Add a hierarchical prior over concept families to model transfer, and evaluate the predictions on GINC-style synthetic data with controlled concept separation.
items.pad_claim: Within the Bayesian-inference model of in-context learning, derive the rate at which the implicit posterior concentrates on the shared latent concept as a function of the divergence between concept-conditioned distributions in the pretraining mixture, predicting faster accuracy gains (fewer examples) when concepts are well separated. Add a hierarchical prior over concept families to model transfer, and evaluate the predictions on GINC-style synthetic data with controlled concept separation.

items.base_claim: Holding model size and training data fixed, sweep BPE tokenizer granularity (varying only merge count / vocabulary size, with one arm forcing single-digit number pretokenization) and, at each setting, measure token fertility per language, multi-digit arithmetic accuracy by operand length, and downstream multilingual task scores. Report the fertility-accuracy correlation across the grid and how the arithmetic-optimal and multilingual-optimal granularities trade off.
items.sig_claim: Holding model size and training data fixed, sweep BPE tokenizer granularity (varying only merge count / vocabulary size, with one arm forcing single-digit number pretokenization) and, at each setting, measure token fertility per language, multi-digit arithmetic accuracy by operand length, and downstream multilingual task scores. Report the fertility-accuracy correlation across the grid and how the arithmetic-optimal and multilingual-optimal granularities trade off.
items.pad_claim: Holding model size and training data fixed, sweep BPE tokenizer granularity (varying only merge count / vocabulary size, with one arm forcing single-digit number pretokenization) and, at each setting, measure token fertility per language, multi-digit arithmetic accuracy by operand length, and downstream multilingual task scores. Report the fertility-accuracy correlation across the grid and how the arithmetic-optimal and multilingual-optimal granularities trade off.

items.base_claim: In CLIP-based open-vocabulary detection, represent each category by averaging the CLIP text embeddings of an ensemble of prompt templates plus synonyms and hypernyms drawn from a lexical database, and train the region classifier using hard-negative category names semantically close to the ground-truth label. Region embeddings are still distilled from a frozen CLIP image encoder, and classification remains cosine similarity between a region feature and the aggregated category text embedding.
items.sig_claim: In CLIP-based open-vocabulary detection, represent each category by averaging the CLIP text embeddings of an ensemble of prompt templates plus synonyms and hypernyms drawn from a lexical database, and train the region classifier using hard-negative category names semantically close to the ground-truth label. Region embeddings are still distilled from a frozen CLIP image encoder, and classification remains cosine similarity between a region feature and the aggregated category text embedding.
items.pad_claim: In CLIP-based open-vocabulary detection, represent each category by averaging the CLIP text embeddings of an ensemble of prompt templates plus synonyms and hypernyms drawn from a lexical database, and train the region classifier using hard-negative category names semantically close to the ground-truth label. Region embeddings are still distilled from a frozen CLIP image encoder, and classification remains cosine similarity between a region feature and the aggregated category text embedding.

items.base_claim: Replace PairNorm's single global rescaling constant with a normalization target (for total pairwise squared distance across nodes) that varies per node-degree bucket and per layer depth, set by a small number of coefficients learned during training. The underlying graph aggregation operator is left unchanged.
items.sig_claim: Replace PairNorm's single global rescaling constant with a normalization target that varies per node-degree bucket and per layer depth, controlled by a small set of coefficients fit during training, while leaving the aggregation operator unchanged.
items.pad_claim: Replace the PairNorm normalization layer's single global rescaling constant with a rescaling factor / target for total pairwise squared distance that varies with node degree and layer depth, set per degree bucket and per layer via a small number of learned coefficients, leaving the aggregation operator itself untouched.

items.base_claim: Augment a GIN's input node features with fixed precomputed structural descriptors (e.g., counts of small subgraphs such as triangles and short cycles, plus shortest-path distance encodings to a set of anchor nodes); characterize which descriptor sets provably raise distinguishing power above 1-WL for named graph families, and measure the gains on standard graph-classification benchmarks.
items.sig_claim: Augment a GIN's node features with a fixed set of structural descriptors (counts of small subgraphs like triangles and short cycles, plus shortest-path distance encodings to anchor nodes); give a per-graph-family account of which descriptor sets provably lift distinguishing power above 1-WL, and measure realized gains on standard graph-classification benchmarks.
items.pad_claim: Add to each GIN node a fixed vector of precomputed structural descriptors (counts of small subgraphs such as triangles and short cycles, plus shortest-path distance encodings to chosen anchor nodes) before message passing; determine for named graph families which descriptor sets provably raise distinguishing power beyond 1-WL, and quantify improvements on standard graph-classification benchmarks.

items.base_claim: Replace DP-SGD's single global gradient-clipping norm with per-layer clipping norms, each set proportional to a running online estimate of that layer's average per-example gradient norm; calibrate the added Gaussian noise separately per layer to its clipping norm, and compose the per-layer mechanisms under the moments accountant to report a total (epsilon, delta), at matched total noise versus standard DP-SGD.
items.sig_claim: Replace DP-SGD's single global clipping norm with a vector of per-layer clipping norms, each proportional to a running online estimate of that layer's average per-example gradient norm; calibrate Gaussian noise separately per layer and compose the per-layer mechanisms under the moments accountant to report one total (epsilon, delta) at matched total noise.
items.pad_claim: Replace DP-SGD's single global clipping norm with per-layer clipping norms, each proportional to a running online estimate of that layer's average per-example gradient norm; calibrate the additive Gaussian noise separately per layer to its assigned clipping norm and compose the per-layer mechanisms under the moments accountant to report a single total (epsilon, delta), holding total noise identical to standard DP-SGD.

items.base_claim: Run the LiRA membership-inference attack against image classifiers trained over a sweep of one regularizer's strength (e.g., weight-decay coefficient or dropout rate) with architecture, dataset, and optimization budget held fixed; at each setting measure attack true-positive rate at fixed low false-positive rates and the train-test generalization gap, then fit the functional form relating regularization strength to attack success and identify the strength at which marginal privacy gains diminish.
items.sig_claim: Run LiRA against image classifiers while sweeping a single regularizer's strength (weight-decay coefficient or dropout rate), holding architecture, dataset, and optimization budget fixed; record attack true-positive rate at fixed low false-positive rates and the train-test generalization gap per setting, fit the functional form linking regularization strength to attack success, and locate the point of diminishing marginal privacy return.
items.pad_claim: Run LiRA against image classifiers trained across a graded sweep of one regularizer's strength (weight-decay coefficient or dropout rate), with architecture, dataset, and optimization budget fixed; at each setting read out attack true-positive rate at fixed low false-positive rates plus the train-test generalization gap, fit the curve relating regularization strength to attack success, and identify where its marginal effect diminishes.

items.base_claim: Instantiate the X-learner for conditional-average-treatment-effect estimation as a single shared-trunk neural network: one shared representation feeds two outcome heads (treated and control), two further heads regress the first-stage imputed individual effects, and the two effect estimates are combined using an estimated propensity score, with an integral-probability-metric penalty balancing the treated and control representations; the whole model is trained end-to-end rather than in separate stages.
items.sig_claim: Build the X-learner as one jointly optimized neural network: a single shared trunk feeds two outcome heads for treated and control responses, two further heads regress the first-stage imputed individual effects, the two effect estimates are combined through an estimated propensity score, and an integral-probability-metric penalty enforces balance between treated and control representations, all trained end-to-end.
items.pad_claim: Instantiate the X-learner on a single shared-trunk network: one learned representation feeds two outcome heads (treated and control), two further heads regress the first-stage imputed individual effects, the two effect estimates are combined via an estimated propensity score, and an integral-probability-metric penalty aligns the treated and control representations; the entire model is trained end-to-end instead of as separate stages.

items.base_claim: Compute the double machine learning orthogonal-score estimate over many independent random cross-fitting partitions and aggregate the point estimates with a median-of-means combiner. Modify the variance estimator to add between-partition variability to the usual within-partition sandwich term, and fit each fold's nuisance functions with a cross-validated ensemble of learners selected by out-of-fold loss; the estimand, orthogonal moment, and asymptotics are unchanged.
items.sig_claim: Compute the double machine learning orthogonal-score estimate over many independent random cross-fitting partitions and aggregate the point estimates with a median-of-means combiner. Modify the variance estimator to add between-partition variability to the usual within-partition sandwich term, and fit each fold's nuisance functions with a cross-validated ensemble of learners selected by out-of-fold loss; the estimand, orthogonal moment, and asymptotics are unchanged.
items.pad_claim: Compute the double machine learning orthogonal-score estimate over many independent random cross-fitting partitions and aggregate the point estimates with a median-of-means combiner. Modify the variance estimator to add between-partition variability to the usual within-partition sandwich term, and fit each fold's nuisance functions with a cross-validated ensemble of learners selected by out-of-fold loss; the estimand, orthogonal moment, and asymptotics are unchanged.

items.base_claim: Replace DLinear's single channel-shared linear map with several channel-grouped linear heads, partitioning channels by correlation-based clustering and giving each group its own trend and seasonal weight matrices while keeping the moving-average seasonal-trend decomposition and direct multi-step output. Evaluate on ETT, Electricity, Traffic, and Weather against DLinear and Informer, reporting MSE and MAE across horizons.
items.sig_claim: Replace DLinear's single channel-shared linear map with several channel-grouped linear heads, partitioning channels by correlation-based clustering and giving each group its own trend and seasonal weight matrices while keeping the moving-average seasonal-trend decomposition and direct multi-step output. Evaluate on ETT, Electricity, Traffic, and Weather against DLinear and Informer, reporting MSE and MAE across horizons.
items.pad_claim: Replace DLinear's single channel-shared linear map with several channel-grouped linear heads, partitioning channels by correlation-based clustering and giving each group its own trend and seasonal weight matrices while keeping the moving-average seasonal-trend decomposition and direct multi-step output. Evaluate on ETT, Electricity, Traffic, and Weather against DLinear and Informer, reporting MSE and MAE across horizons.

items.base_claim: Extend a Chronos-style tokenized forecaster to condition on exogenous covariates by scaling and quantizing each covariate channel with the same binning scheme as the target, interleaving the covariate tokens with target tokens along the sequence and adding channel-identifying information to the embeddings. Fine-tune the backbone on a mixed corpus of series with aligned covariates, leaving the tokenizer and next-token objective unchanged; at inference supply known future covariates as context and decode targets autoregressively. Evaluate zero-shot accuracy with versus without covariates.
items.sig_claim: Extend a Chronos-style tokenized forecaster to condition on exogenous covariates by scaling and quantizing each covariate channel with the same binning scheme as the target, interleaving the covariate tokens with target tokens along the sequence and adding channel-identifying information to the embeddings. Fine-tune the backbone on a mixed corpus of series with aligned covariates, leaving the tokenizer and next-token objective unchanged; at inference supply known future covariates as context and decode targets autoregressively. Evaluate zero-shot accuracy with versus without covariates.
items.pad_claim: Extend a Chronos-style tokenized forecaster to condition on exogenous covariates by scaling and quantizing each covariate channel with the same binning scheme as the target, interleaving the covariate tokens with target tokens along the sequence and adding channel-identifying information to the embeddings. Fine-tune the backbone on a mixed corpus of series with aligned covariates, leaving the tokenizer and next-token objective unchanged; at inference supply known future covariates as context and decode targets autoregressively. Evaluate zero-shot accuracy with versus without covariates.

items.base_claim: Extend first-order MAML and Reptile with per-layer inner-loop learning rates that are meta-learned jointly with the shared initialization: during adaptation each layer's parameters are updated by first-order task gradients scaled by that layer's learned step size, and the outer loop optimizes both the initialization and the vector of step sizes on post-adaptation loss. Evaluate on standard few-shot classification benchmarks against fixed-step baselines.
items.sig_claim: Extend first-order MAML and Reptile with per-layer inner-loop learning rates that are meta-learned jointly with the shared initialization: during adaptation each layer's parameters are updated by first-order task gradients scaled by that layer's learned step size, and the outer loop optimizes both the initialization and the vector of step sizes on post-adaptation loss. Evaluate on standard few-shot classification benchmarks against fixed-step baselines.
items.pad_claim: Extend first-order MAML and Reptile with per-layer inner-loop learning rates that are meta-learned jointly with the shared initialization: during adaptation each layer's parameters are updated by first-order task gradients scaled by that layer's learned step size, and the outer loop optimizes both the initialization and the vector of step sizes on post-adaptation loss. Evaluate on standard few-shot classification benchmarks against fixed-step baselines.

items.base_claim: Combine EWC's quadratic Fisher penalty with a small experience-replay buffer in one training objective: each step trains on current-task data plus a minibatch sampled from the buffer while retaining the EWC anchoring term on parameters important to past tasks, and the buffer is also used to re-estimate the Fisher importance weights online rather than only at task boundaries. Tune the relative weighting of replay loss and regularization penalty per task; evaluate on standard task-incremental benchmarks against each component in isolation.
items.sig_claim: Combine EWC's quadratic Fisher penalty with a small experience-replay buffer in one training objective: each step trains on current-task data plus a minibatch sampled from the buffer while retaining the EWC anchoring term on parameters important to past tasks, and the buffer is also used to re-estimate the Fisher importance weights online rather than only at task boundaries. Tune the relative weighting of replay loss and regularization penalty per task; evaluate on standard task-incremental benchmarks against each component in isolation.
items.pad_claim: Combine EWC's quadratic Fisher penalty with a small experience-replay buffer in one training objective: each step trains on current-task data plus a minibatch sampled from the buffer while retaining the EWC anchoring term on parameters important to past tasks, and the buffer is also used to re-estimate the Fisher importance weights online rather than only at task boundaries. Tune the relative weighting of replay loss and regularization penalty per task; evaluate on standard task-incremental benchmarks against each component in isolation.

items.base_claim: Replace the three exact group-fairness criteria (within-group calibration, false-positive-rate balance, false-negative-rate balance) with epsilon-relaxed versions that permit bounded violations, and derive the feasible region of jointly achievable violation tuples (eps_cal, eps_fpr, eps_fnr) as a function of the between-group base-rate difference and score accuracy. Estimate this tradeoff frontier on COMPAS and Adult, reporting how the minimum forced violation of one criterion grows as the others are tightened.
items.sig_claim: Replace the three exact group-fairness criteria (within-group calibration, false-positive-rate balance, false-negative-rate balance) with epsilon-relaxed versions that permit bounded violations, and derive the feasible region of jointly achievable violation tuples (eps_cal, eps_fpr, eps_fnr) as a function of the between-group base-rate difference and score accuracy. Estimate this tradeoff frontier on COMPAS and Adult, reporting how the minimum forced violation of one criterion grows as the others are tightened.
items.pad_claim: Replace the three exact group-fairness criteria (within-group calibration, false-positive-rate balance, false-negative-rate balance) with epsilon-relaxed versions that permit bounded violations, and derive the feasible region of jointly achievable violation tuples (eps_cal, eps_fpr, eps_fnr) as a function of the between-group base-rate difference and score accuracy. Estimate this tradeoff frontier on COMPAS and Adult, reporting how the minimum forced violation of one criterion grows as the others are tightened.

items.base_claim: Keep dropout active at test time within each member of a deep ensemble so the predictive distribution is a mixture over both independently initialized members and sampled dropout masks. Sweep the number of members and the dropout rate on image-classification benchmarks, measuring calibration (ECE, NLL) and out-of-distribution detection against plain deep ensembles and plain MC-dropout at matched compute.
items.sig_claim: Keep dropout active at test time within each member of a deep ensemble so the predictive distribution is a mixture over both independently initialized members and sampled dropout masks. Sweep the number of members and the dropout rate on image-classification benchmarks, measuring calibration (ECE, NLL) and out-of-distribution detection against plain deep ensembles and plain MC-dropout at matched compute.
items.pad_claim: Keep dropout active at test time within each member of a deep ensemble so the predictive distribution is a mixture over both independently initialized members and sampled dropout masks. Sweep the number of members and the dropout rate on image-classification benchmarks, measuring calibration (ECE, NLL) and out-of-distribution detection against plain deep ensembles and plain MC-dropout at matched compute.

items.base_claim: In inverse-propensity-scoring for implicit-feedback recommendation, replace the single global item-popularity propensity with per-item propensities conditioned on user covariates (activity level, coarse demographic group), fit by a regularized logistic model over item and user features, and stabilize the weights with self-normalization and propensity clipping. Reweight the training loss with these per-user-segment propensities.
items.sig_claim: In inverse-propensity-scoring for implicit-feedback recommendation, replace the single global item-popularity propensity with per-item propensities conditioned on user covariates (activity level, coarse demographic group), fit by a regularized logistic model over item and user features, and stabilize the weights with self-normalization and propensity clipping. Reweight the training loss with these per-user-segment propensities.
items.pad_claim: In inverse-propensity-scoring for implicit-feedback recommendation, replace the single global item-popularity propensity with per-item propensities conditioned on user covariates (activity level, coarse demographic group), fit by a regularized logistic model over item and user features, and stabilize the weights with self-normalization and propensity clipping. Reweight the training loss with these per-user-segment propensities.

items.base_claim: Build a vision-language connector that runs a Q-Former (emitting a fixed set of compressed query tokens) and an MLP per-patch projection (emitting the full set of per-patch tokens) in parallel, weights the two token streams with a learned scalar gate, and concatenates them before prepending to the text. Train the module on image-text pairs with the vision encoder and language model frozen.
items.sig_claim: Build a vision-language connector that runs a Q-Former (emitting a fixed set of compressed query tokens) and an MLP per-patch projection (emitting the full set of per-patch tokens) in parallel, weights the two token streams with a learned scalar gate, and concatenates them before prepending to the text. Train the module on image-text pairs with the vision encoder and language model frozen.
items.pad_claim: Build a vision-language connector that runs a Q-Former (emitting a fixed set of compressed query tokens) and an MLP per-patch projection (emitting the full set of per-patch tokens) in parallel, weights the two token streams with a learned scalar gate, and concatenates them before prepending to the text. Train the module on image-text pairs with the vision encoder and language model frozen.

items.base_claim: In the DARTS supernet, normalize each candidate operation's output to unit variance using running statistics before forming the architecture-weighted mixture, so all operations contribute at comparable magnitude, and add a small entropy penalty on the architecture-parameter distribution to slow premature collapse. Evaluate by the improvement in correlation between supernet-derived ranking and standalone accuracy.
items.sig_claim: In the DARTS supernet, normalize each candidate operation's output to unit variance using running statistics before forming the architecture-weighted mixture, so all operations contribute at comparable magnitude, and add a small entropy penalty on the architecture-parameter distribution to slow premature collapse. Evaluate by the improvement in correlation between supernet-derived ranking and standalone accuracy.
items.pad_claim: In the DARTS supernet, normalize each candidate operation's output to unit variance using running statistics before forming the architecture-weighted mixture, so all operations contribute at comparable magnitude, and add a small entropy penalty on the architecture-parameter distribution to slow premature collapse. Evaluate by the improvement in correlation between supernet-derived ranking and standalone accuracy.

items.base_claim: Combine GPTQ's Hessian-guided post-training weight quantization with outlier-channel protection: from calibration activation statistics, identify the highest-magnitude activation channels and keep their weight columns in higher precision, then quantize the remaining columns to 4-bit using GPTQ's second-order error compensation that accounts for the fixed high-precision columns. A per-layer activation-magnitude threshold selects the protected fraction, which is tuned to trade model size against accuracy.
items.sig_claim: Combine GPTQ's Hessian-guided post-training weight quantization with outlier-channel protection: from calibration activation statistics, identify the highest-magnitude activation channels and keep their weight columns in higher precision, then quantize the remaining columns to 4-bit using GPTQ's second-order error compensation that accounts for the fixed high-precision columns. A per-layer activation-magnitude threshold selects the protected fraction, which is tuned to trade model size against accuracy.
items.pad_claim: Combine GPTQ's Hessian-guided post-training weight quantization with outlier-channel protection: from calibration activation statistics, identify the highest-magnitude activation channels and keep their weight columns in higher precision, then quantize the remaining columns to 4-bit using GPTQ's second-order error compensation that accounts for the fixed high-precision columns. A per-layer activation-magnitude threshold selects the protected fraction, which is tuned to trade model size against accuracy.

items.base_claim: Perform structured pruning of whole convolutional channels and attention heads at a fixed parameter budget, scoring each group by a movement-based importance (the summed tendency of its weights to grow in magnitude during fine-tuning) and removing the lowest-scoring groups, using iterative pruning with weight rewinding to the original initialization. Compare this against scoring groups by aggregated weight magnitude (group L2 norm), reporting task accuracy and measured inference latency at matched sparsity on vision and language backbones.
items.sig_claim: Perform structured pruning of whole convolutional channels and attention heads at a fixed parameter budget, scoring each group by a movement-based importance (the summed tendency of its weights to grow in magnitude during fine-tuning) and removing the lowest-scoring groups, using iterative pruning with weight rewinding to the original initialization. Compare this against scoring groups by aggregated weight magnitude (group L2 norm), reporting task accuracy and measured inference latency at matched sparsity on vision and language backbones.
items.pad_claim: Perform structured pruning of whole convolutional channels and attention heads at a fixed parameter budget, scoring each group by a movement-based importance (the summed tendency of its weights to grow in magnitude during fine-tuning) and removing the lowest-scoring groups, using iterative pruning with weight rewinding to the original initialization. Compare this against scoring groups by aggregated weight magnitude (group L2 norm), reporting task accuracy and measured inference latency at matched sparsity on vision and language backbones.

items.base_claim: Under a fixed LoRA adapter-parameter budget, set each adapted layer's low-rank adapter rank in proportion to a cheap spectral statistic of its pretrained weight matrix (e.g., stable rank or spectral energy) computed once before training, rounding ranks to meet the budget, with the scaling factor and rest of the LoRA parameterization unchanged. Compare this allocation against uniform-rank baselines at matched parameter counts.
items.sig_claim: Under a fixed LoRA adapter-parameter budget, set each adapted layer's low-rank adapter rank in proportion to a cheap spectral statistic of its pretrained weight matrix (e.g., stable rank or spectral energy) computed once before training, rounding ranks to meet the budget, with the scaling factor and rest of the LoRA parameterization unchanged. Compare this allocation against uniform-rank baselines at matched parameter counts.
items.pad_claim: Under a fixed LoRA adapter-parameter budget, set each adapted layer's low-rank adapter rank in proportion to a cheap spectral statistic of its pretrained weight matrix (e.g., stable rank or spectral energy) computed once before training, rounding ranks to meet the budget, with the scaling factor and rest of the LoRA parameterization unchanged. Compare this allocation against uniform-rank baselines at matched parameter counts.

items.base_claim: In forward loss correction for class-conditional label noise, re-estimate the noise transition matrix at fixed intervals during training (from the current model's high-confidence per-class anchor examples) rather than once from a warm-up model, updating it as an exponential moving average of successive estimates and computing the forward-corrected loss with this running matrix. Only the estimation schedule and its stabilization change.
items.sig_claim: In forward loss correction for class-conditional label noise, re-estimate the noise transition matrix at fixed intervals during training (from the current model's high-confidence per-class anchor examples) rather than once from a warm-up model, updating it as an exponential moving average of successive estimates and computing the forward-corrected loss with this running matrix. Only the estimation schedule and its stabilization change.
items.pad_claim: In forward loss correction for class-conditional label noise, re-estimate the noise transition matrix at fixed intervals during training (from the current model's high-confidence per-class anchor examples) rather than once from a warm-up model, updating it as an exponential moving average of successive estimates and computing the forward-corrected loss with this running matrix. Only the estimation schedule and its stabilization change.

items.base_claim: For out-of-distribution detection, compute the energy score from a trained classifier's logits, then estimate per-class mean and standard deviation of that energy over in-distribution training inputs assigned to each class; at test time, standardize an input's energy using the statistics of its predicted class and threshold the standardized value. Requires only stored per-class statistics, with no retraining or auxiliary outlier data.
items.sig_claim: For out-of-distribution detection, compute the energy score from a trained classifier's logits, then estimate per-class mean and standard deviation of that energy over in-distribution training inputs assigned to each class; at test time, standardize an input's energy using the statistics of its predicted class and threshold the standardized value. Requires only stored per-class statistics, with no retraining or auxiliary outlier data.
items.pad_claim: For out-of-distribution detection, compute the energy score from a trained classifier's logits, then estimate per-class mean and standard deviation of that energy over in-distribution training inputs assigned to each class; at test time, standardize an input's energy using the statistics of its predicted class and threshold the standardized value. Requires only stored per-class statistics, with no retraining or auxiliary outlier data.
